description: Real-world career and application connections for Grade 1 Living Things curriculum

careers.name: Veterinarian (Vet)
careers.description: A doctor who helps sick animals get better
careers.connection: Students learn that animals need food and care - vets help animals stay healthy

careers.name: Zookeeper
careers.description: Someone who takes care of animals at the zoo
careers.connection: Learning about animal needs (food, water, shelter) connects to how zookeepers feed and care for animals

careers.name: Farmer
careers.description: Someone who grows food and cares for farm animals
careers.connection: Plants need water and sunlight to grow - farmers plant seeds and help crops grow

careers.name: Gardener
careers.description: Someone who grows plants and flowers
careers.connection: Learning about what plants need (water, sun, soil) - gardeners help plants grow

careers.name: Pet Store Worker
careers.description: Someone who helps take care of pets and tells families how to care for them
careers.connection: Connecting animal needs to how we care for pets at home

careers.name: Biologist (Scientist who studies living things)
careers.description: A scientist who learns about plants and animals
careers.connection: Scientists observe and learn about living things just like students do on nature walks

applications.title: Planting a Classroom Garden
applications.description: Students plant seeds and observe how living things grow over time
applications.curriculum_link: Basic needs of plants (water, sunlight, soil)

applications.title: Nature Walk Observation
applications.description: Walk around school grounds to find and name living things
applications.curriculum_link: Identify and name living things in local environment

applications.title: Pet Care Discussion
applications.description: Discuss what pets need (food, water, love) and how we help them
applications.curriculum_link: Living things need food and respond to environment

applications.title: Animal Movement Game
applications.description: Act out how different animals move (walk, fly, swim, crawl)

applications.title: My Pet or Favorite Animal Project
applications.description: Students share about a pet or animal they know, what it needs, and how it moves
applications.curriculum_link: Animals need food, water, shelter; different movement types
